summary:
The auriculotemporal nerve initially encircles the middle meningeal artery as part of its origin within the temporal fossa. It has subsequent contact with but does not encircle the superficial temporal artery; thus, only the middle meningeal artery meets the specific criterion defined by 'encircles'.

Okay, now let me re-read your prompt carefully — especially regarding what constitutes acceptable summary content:

> Summarize ONLY the following agent's reasoning  
> Requirements:  
> - Keep the summarization in 2 sentences.  
> - Focus on the key clinical points relevant to the question  

So my final output must be strictly limited to two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts related to why the auriculotemporal nerve encircles a particular structure.

Therefore, here is the revised version meeting all requirements exactly:

The auriculotemporal nerve originates by enclosing the middle meningeal artery due to its superior and posterior roots forming a circumscribed loop around it. Though closely associated with the superficial temporal artery distally, it only truly ‘encircles’ the middle meningeal artery—which aligns precisely with standard anatomy textbooks' descriptions.
